celebration is often called the largest outside of New Orleans, butrepresentatives say they've never made that claim.PublishedFebruary 24, 2022", "relevance_score": 0.5}, {"title": "Mardi Gras: a beginner\u2019s guide to the biggest party of the year", "url": "https://www.trafalgar.com/real-word/mardi-gras/", "snippet": "[Jina Extracted Content] Title: Mardi Gras: a beginner\u2019s guide to the biggest party of the year URL Source: https://www.trafalgar.com/real-word/mardi-gras/ Published Time: 2025-03-18T16:49:35+00:00 Markdown Content: The vibes are high, beads are flying, and you\u2019re grooving to the jazzy tunes you\u2019ll find in New Orleans. The smell of beignets punctures the air, glitter is everywhere and golden doubloons are flying. It\u2019s [Mardi Gras](https://www.mardigrasneworleans.com/), the biggest, wildest, and most colorful party! But what exactly is Mardi Gras, and what can a first-timer expect? It\u2019s more than just one day of partying \u2013it\u2019s a whole season of parades, traditions, and celebrations with plenty of history. You need to know when to go, where to stand and what to wear, so we\u2019ve written this crash course on all things Mardi Gras. Read on and get ready! Contents 1.   [1. What is Mardi Gras?](https://www.trafalgar.com/real-word/mardi-gras/#h-1-what-is-mardi-gras) 2.   [2. Where is Mardi Gras celebrated?](https://www.trafalgar.com/real-word/mardi-gras/#h-2-where-is-mardi-gras-celebrated) 3.   [3. When is Mardi Gras (and how long does it last) in 2026?](https://www.trafalgar.com/real-word/mardi-gras/#h-3-when-is-mardi-gras-and-how-long-does-it-last-in-2026) 4.   [4. When to Visit New Orleans for Mardi Gras 2026?](https://www.trafalgar.com/real-word/mardi-gras/#h-4-when-to-visit-new-orleans-for-mardi-gras-2026) 5.   [5. Mardi Gras parades & traditions in New Orleans](https://www.trafalgar.com/real-word/mardi-gras/#h-5-mardi-gras-parades-amp-traditions-in-new-orleans) 6.   [6. What to wear to Mardi Gras?](https://www.trafalgar.com/real-word/mardi-gras/#h-6-what-to-wear-to-mardi-gras) 7.   [7. Final tips for first-timers](https://www.trafalgar.com/real-word/mardi-gras/#h-7-final-tips-for-first-timers) ## 1. What is Mardi Gras? ![Image 1: You can't miss the huge floats and parades at Mardi Gras in New Orleans](https://www.trafalgar.com/real-word/wp-content/uploads//2025/03/parade-mardi-gras-1024x576.jpg)\n...\nTitle: Mardi Gras: a beginner\u2019s guide to the biggest party of the year URL Source: https://www.trafalgar.com/real-word/mardi-gras/ Published Time: 2025-03-18T16:49:35+00:00 Markdown Content: The vibes are high, beads are flying, and you\u2019re grooving to the jazzy tunes you\u2019ll find in New Orleans. The smell of beignets punctures the air, glitter is everywhere and golden doubloons are flying. It\u2019s [Mardi Gras](https://www.mardigrasneworleans.com/), the biggest, wildest, and most colorful party! But what exactly is Mardi Gras, and what can a first-timer expect? It\u2019s more than just one day of partying \u2013it\u2019s a whole season of parades, traditions, and celebrations with plenty of history. You need to know when to go, where to stand and what to wear, so we\u2019ve written this crash course on all things Mardi Gras. Read on and get ready! Contents 1.   [1. What is Mardi Gras?](https://www.trafalgar.com/real-word/mardi-gras/#h-1-what-is-mardi-gras) 2.   [2. Where is Mardi Gras celebrated?](https:", "relevance_score": 0.8}, {"title": "Largest Mardi Gras celebrations beyond New Orleans - Tripadvisor", "url": "https://www.tripadvisor.in/Articles-lFpsUf3OpRSA-Largest_mardi_gras_celebrations.html", "snippet": "[Jina Extracted Content] Title: tripadvisor.in URL Source: https://www.tripadvisor.in/Articles-lFpsUf3OpRSA-Largest_mardi_gras_celebrations.html Warning: Target URL returned error 403: Forbidden Warning: This page maybe not yet fully loaded, consider explicitly specify a timeout. Markdown Content:", "relevance_score": 0.8}, {"title": "17 Mardi Gras Facts You Need to Know About - Insight Vacations", "url": "https://www.insightvacations.com/blog/mardi-gras-facts/", "snippet": "[Jina Extracted Content] Title: 17 Mardi Gras Facts You Need to Know About URL Source: https://www.insightvacations.com/blog/mardi-gras-facts/ Published Time: 2025-03-13T13:01:22+00:00 Markdown Content: ![Image 1: IV_Summer-Sale_Standard_Horizontal_Logo](https://www.insightvacations.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/IV_Summer-Sale_Standard_Horizontal_Logo.png) ## Save up to $3,400 per couple and enjoy a $300 flight credit Laissez les bon temps rouler (\u201clet the good times roll\u201d) during Mardi Gras this year. Carnival season is in full swing around the world from the beginning of January until the start of Lent and one of the most popular aspects of Carnival is Mardi Gras: the final Tuesday of the season. But did you know that there\u2019s an official King Baby sports mascot or that there\u2019s one place in New Orleans where you can see parade floats year-round? Whether you\u2019re headed to [New Orleans](https://www.insightvacations.com/en-gb/tours/country-roads-of-the-deep-south?optionId=1111), the epicenter of Mardi Gras in the [United States](https://www.insightvacations.com/destinations/usa-and-canada/united-states), or are celebrating Carnival\u2019s conclusion in another city like Mobile, St. Louis, or Lake Charles, take a bit of trivia along with you \u2013 with these 17 insightful facts about Mardi Gras. ## 1. You\u2019re not the first person to wear that string of beads Millions upon millions of \u2018favors\u2019 are thrown from the floats during the festival, particularly beads. The beads have become one of the most iconic items associated with the celebration. The official colors of Mardi Gras are purple, green, and gold and they represent justice, faith, and power; and perhaps inadvertently symbolically, you won\u2019t find virginal white anywhere on the string. It\u2019s impossible to say just how many beads are brand new, but one thing is for sure: Mardi Gras parades recycle beads from year to year. Many locals collect beads and drop them off at community offices that clean and prepare to reuse them the following year. ## 2. Mask wearing holds a special significance in the U.S. Look at photographs from any Carnival celebrations around the world and you\u2019ll see festival goers donning flamboyant masks. A parade in New Orleans is no different, though there\u2019s a special significance to the mask wearing in the American South. When racial inequality prevented African Americans from joining the Carnival celebrations, many used masks and costumes to attend the event. Interestingly enough, it\u2019s against Louisiana law to wear masks, hoods, or any type of facial disguise in public, with one exception being Mardi Gras festivities. In fact, all float riders _must_ wear a mask or paint their faces according to state law. ## 3. It\u2019s just a day, not an entire season Mardi Gras is such a popular event across the United States that many commercial festivities refer to the time as a \u201cMardi Gras season\u201d. While the festivities may certainly be similar from day to day, there\u2019s a big difference between Mardi Gras and Carnival.
